examples.title: Pseudocode: OSI Security Model Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates how to implement the OSI security model with threat classification and countermeasures at each layer
examples.code:
ALGORITHM OSI_Security_Check
INPUT: network_packet, current_layer
OUTPUT: security_action, threat_level

BEGIN
  SET threat_types = ["interception", "interruption", "modification", "fabrication"]
  
  FUNCTION Classify_Threat(packet, layer):
    SWITCH layer:
      CASE 1: // Physical Layer
        IF packet.physical_access_unauthorized THEN
          RETURN "interception", HIGH
        ENDIF
        
      CASE 2: // Data Link Layer  
        IF packet.mac_spoofed OR packet.vlan_hopping THEN
          RETURN "interception", HIGH
        ENDIF
        
      CASE 3: // Network Layer
        IF packet.ip_spoofed OR packet.routing_attack THEN
          RETURN ("interception" OR "modification"), HIGH
        ENDIF
        
      CASE 4: // Transport Layer
        IF packet.syn_flood OR packet.session_hijacked THEN
          RETURN "interruption", CRITICAL
        ENDIF
        
      CASE 5: // Session Layer
        IF packet.session_stolen OR packet.cookie_tampered THEN
          RETURN "interception", HIGH
        ENDIF
        
      CASE 6: // Presentation Layer
        IF NOT packet.encrypted THEN
          RETURN "interception", MEDIUM
        ENDIF
        
      CASE 7: // Application Layer
        IF packet.sql_injection OR packet.xss_detected THEN
          RETURN ("modification" OR "fabrication"), CRITICAL
        ENDIF
    ENDSWITCH
    
  FUNCTION Apply_Countermeasure(threat, layer):
    SWITCH layer:
      CASE 1: RETURN "secure_facility", "lock_device"
      CASE 2: RETURN "802.1X", "VLAN_isolation"
      CASE 3: RETURN "ACL_filter", "secure_routing"
      CASE 4: RETURN "TLS", "firewall"
      CASE 5: RETURN "SSL_session", "secure_cookie"
      CASE 6: RETURN "AES_encryption", "secure_compression"
      CASE 7: RETURN "HTTPS", "input_validation"
    ENDSWITCH
    
  // Main processing
  SET threat, level = Classify_Threat(network_packet, current_layer)
  SET actions = Apply_Countermeasure(threat, current_layer)
  
  IF level >= HIGH THEN
    LOG_ALERT(threat, current_layer, actions)
    BLOCK_PACKET()
  ELSE IF level == MEDIUM
    LOG_WARNING(threat, current_layer)
    MONITOR_PACKET()
  ENDIF
  
  RETURN actions, level
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the OSI Security Model by classifying network threats at each of the 7 OSI layers and applying appropriate countermeasures. It shows how interception, interruption, modification, and fabrication threats can be detected and handled at different network layers.

examples.title: Python: Network Security Threat Classifier
examples.description: Implements a network security model that classifies threats and applies appropriate security measures based on OSI layers
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the Network Security Model by creating classes for threat classification at each OSI layer. It shows how different security threats (interception, interruption, modification, fabrication) are handled at each layer with appropriate countermeasures. The NetworkSegmentation class demonstrates how networks can be divided into isolated segments with different security policies.

examples.title: C: Simple Packet Filter Firewall
examples.description: Implements a basic firewall with packet filtering based on source/destination IP, ports, and protocols
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates a basic packet filter firewall that embodies the Network Security Model. It includes firewall rules, threat classification based on OSI layers, and demonstrates how different security threats (interception, interruption, modification) are identified and handled. The program shows practical implementation of network segmentation concepts and the OSI security model at the transport and application layers.
